title: BlackSky Technology (BKSY) Posts Heavy Q1 Losses That Test High-Growth Bullish Narratives
text: BlackSky Technology reported challenging Q1 2026 results with revenue of approximately $20.8 million and a loss per share of roughly $0.82. The company's net loss excluding extra items reached $29.7 million, while the stock traded at $32.24 per share. Over the past year, the company generated approximately $97.8 million in revenue but posted a net loss of roughly $87.1 million, translating to a basic loss per share of about $2.50 for that period. The stock trades at a price-to-sales ratio of 14.1x, significantly above the peer average of 1.6x and the broader Professional Services industry level of 1.1x. This premium pricing reflects investor expectations for future growth despite current unprofitability. Analysts project the company should trade near $32.63, nearly identical to the current price. However, a discounted cash flow analysis suggests a fairer value of approximately $55.84, creating a notable divergence in valuation methodologies. Consensus forecasts approximately 19.4% to 28.4% annual revenue growth over the next three years. Despite this optimism, analysts expect the company to remain unprofitable for at least three years while losses have narrowed at roughly 26.9% annually over five years.
